AssociatedWith: It is associated with Greek painter Michael Damaskinos.
It is associated with the Cretan school of painting.
It is associated with Italian mannerisms.
It is associated with Parmigianino.
It is associated with Raphael.
It is associated with the Disputation of the Holy Sacrament.
It is associated with Transubstantiation.
It is associated with the deesis form.
It is associated with the Cretan School.
It is associated with the Heptanese School.
ConsistsOf: It consists of egg tempera.
It consists of gold leaf.
It consists of a wood panel.
It consists of Christ floating on a cloud.
It consists of the Virgin Mary.
It consists of John the Baptist.
It consists of four evangelists in animal form.
It consists of two angels at Jesus's legs.
It consists of an all-seeing eye.
It consists of six angels in the upper portion.
CreatedBy: It was created by Michael Damaskinos.
CreatedTo: It was created to be a version of Raphael's masterpiece.
HasA: It has a height of 54 cm.
It has a width of 45.5 cm.
It has some degree of damage.
It has the column of scourging.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the traditional deesis form.
It is characterized by the four evangelists in animal form.
It is characterized by the symbols of the holy communion.
It is characterized by the representation of figures in a three-dimensional style.
It is characterized by the symbols of the passion.
HeldIn: It was held in Platytera Monastery in Corfu, Greece.
It was held in the Byzantine and Christian Museum.
It was held in the National Gallery of Athens.
It was held in a private collection in Kefalonia.
It was held in the Church of Evangelistria in Kastro Kefalonia.
It was held in the Byzantine Museum of Ioannina.
It was held in the Byzantine & Christian Museum in Athens.
It was held in the State Hermitage Museum in Saint Petersburg, Russia.
It was held in the Benaki Museum.
It was held in the Abou Adal Icon Collection.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Italian mannerisms.
IsA: It is a work of art.
KnownFor: It is known for being heavily reproduced.
It is known for its similar theme and characteristics to Damaskino's masterpieces.
MemberOf: It is a member of the Cretan school of painting.
It is a member of the Byzantine and Christian Museum collection.
It is a member of the National Gallery of Athens collection.
It is a member of the Byzantine & Christian Museum collection.
It is a member of the Abou Adal Icon Collection.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Italy.

It occurred in Corfu, Greece.

PartOf: It is part of the collection of the Byzantine and Christian Museum.

It is used as a symbol of Saint Matthew.
It is used as a symbol of Saint Luke.
It is used as a symbol of holy communion.

It is used in the Heptanese School.
